name: blood_bank_expiring_units_research_immunology
premise: Blood center medical director Dr. Omar Haddad must decide whether to divert forty-eight near-expiry platelet units to an immunology study that could improve alloimmunization protocols nationwide. Transfusion chief Dr. Lena Cho argues rural hospitals already booked those units for oncology patients arriving tonight. The study protocol allows only fresh-draw windows; delaying research voids a federal grant that funds cold-chain upgrades. Redirecting units may strand documented patients while advancing science that helps thousands later.

agents.name: Dr. Omar Haddad
agents.identity: I am the blood center medical director balancing inventory stewardship, research commitments signed by the board, and equitable distribution contracts with rural hospitals.
agents.goal: I want research that saves future patients without breaking trust with hospitals that scheduled platelets around these lots.

agents.name: Dr. Lena Cho
agents.identity: I am the transfusion service chief who matches platelets to refractory oncology patients with antibody complexity and narrow compatibility.
agents.goal: I want every booked unit to reach a named patient tonight rather than expiring in a lab arm that could reschedule with grant flexibility.

choices: I will divert the near-expiry units to the immunology study under the grant window and pay for emergency courier platelets from a partner region, accepting six-hour delays if couriers miss connections.
I will honor all rural hospital bookings and cancel the study draw for this cycle, accepting grant clawback risk and delayed cold-chain upgrades that affect next summer's inventory.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The research coordinator and oncology charge nurse are both on hold for opposite orders. Dr. Omar Haddad must choose between diverting units to research or honoring rural bookings. What does Dr. Omar Haddad do?
